summary: Central Mine Planning IPO is set to launch on March 20, with a price band of  ₹163-172. The company aims to raise  ₹1,842 crore, benefiting its parent, Coal India, which stands to make a 57,233% gain on its investment.

title: West Asia conflict rattles oil markets, but Citi sees no rush for credit lines
summary: Corporate clients are holding back from drawing bank liquidity despite West Asia tensions, reflecting stronger balance sheets built after the pandemic, says Citi’s global corporate banking chief Jason Rekate.

title: Why Simba beer maker is looking to shift gears to a more premium play
summary: Simba Beer maker Sona Beverages is sharpening its premium focus and expanding into large markets such as Uttar Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh as rising costs squeeze smaller brewers.

title: A  ₹38 lakh hospital bill—and the top-up claim that took months
summary: Top-up policies are a cost-effective way to boost health insurance cover, but deductibles, paperwork and insurer coordination can complicate claims.

title: Paytm shares jumps 4% after rival PhonePe halts IPO plans
summary: Paytm shares jumped on Monday after rival PhonePe paused its IPO plans, easing near-term competition in digital payments. PhonePe cited geopolitical uncertainty and volatile global markets for deferring its listing. The move reflects broader market caution, with fintech firms reevaluating IPO timing and valuations amid heightened investor uncertainty and equity market volatility.

title: FIIs, retail investors increase stakes in 12 midcap stocks in Q3; shares slump up to 40% in 3 months
summary: An analysis of the BSE Mid-Cap Index shows that both FIIs and retail investors increased their stakes in 12 stocks during the December quarter. However, market volatility has weighed on returns, with 11 of these companies delivering negative performance over the past three months and some stocks declining by up to 40%.

title: Fino Payments Bank shares crash 19% on report of possible ED probe; lender clarifies
summary: Fino Payments Bank shares tumbled over 19% to a record low after a report said some online gaming transactions linked to the lender may attract scrutiny from the Enforcement Directorate. The bank later clarified it is not under any ED investigation and is cooperating with DGGI Hyderabad in an ongoing GST-related probe.

title: Sebi sets up working group to review overlap between MF distributors and RIAs
summary: Sebi has formed a working group to eliminate regulatory friction between MFDs and RIAs, and clarify advisory boundaries. This move is part of a broader push to professionalize the sector, standardize advertising, and curb the influence of unregulated market voices.

title: Why Uno Minda stock valuation is getting reset
summary: Uno Minda has an indirect exposure to exports through supplies to its customers or OEMs—contributing more than 90% of revenue.

title: Sensex surges 1,850 points from day's low, Nifty 50 reclaims 23,500— What drove Indian stock market higher? Explained
summary: The Sensex jumped more than 1,850 points from the day's low, while the Nifty 50 reclaimed the 23,500 mark during the session.

title: CMPDI IPO GMP jumps! Coal India-backed Central Mining sees strong demand in grey market ahead of offer launch
summary: Coal India's CMPDI IPO is set to launch with a price band of  ₹163 to  ₹172. The grey market premium indicates potential profitability. The IPO subscription runs from March 20 to 24, with a target of  ₹1,837.8 crore for the company.

title: UPL restructuring to unlock shareholder value through pure-play structure: Report
summary: UPL restructuring will establish a new listed company - UPL Global - combining India and international crop protection businesses. UPL Global is expected to become one of the largest listed crop protection pure-play companies worldwide by revenue.

title: US Fed meeting this week: How Federal Reserve may respond to US-Iran war, rising inflation risks
summary: As the US Federal Reserve prepares for its March meeting, the ongoing US-Iran war and rising inflation risks complicate interest rate predictions. If the US-Iran war drags on, even an interest rate hike later this year cannot be ruled out.

title: US-Iran war: Nifty 50 rebounds 2.35% from today's low; is it the right time to exit gold, silver and buy stocks?
summary: US-Iran war: Despite soaring crude oil prices gold and silver prices are expected to remain sideways due to inflation concerns&nbsp;

title: Ashar Group launches Urban Futures Lab, commits  ₹25 crore to back urbantech startups
summary: Ashar Group has launched Ashar Urban Futures Lab (UFL), India’s first venture studio for urbantech, proptech, and constructiontech, committing  ₹25 crore to support 10–15 startups over the next 24 months.

title: Tax harvesting can save money—but it can also derail your portfolio
summary: As investors rush to optimize taxes before the financial year ends, experts warn that tax harvesting can trigger poor portfolio decisions and behavioural mistakes that undermine long-term returns.
